posts.title: Tunche
posts.text: This was my last project before leaving Leap Game Studio to come to the UK to study at Newcastle University. It is 2D side-scrolling brawler with roguelike elements. The target systems where the PS4 and PC, we had already approached Sony and got some feedback from them, so we were excited about keeping the good work on this one. This project had 2 programmers nearly from the start, I helped on most of the battle system, the level editor tool, the enemies wave editor, enemies AI, animation events, day/night cycle shaders, local multiplayer support and the dialogue system. This was a project I was really sad of leaving because we got positive feedback from different sources, not that the game was perfect or amazing, but that it had potential, and that drove us further every day. The game is still in development and in constant improvement. Technology Used Unity3D Texture Packer

posts.title: Slice And Rise
posts.text: This game, in my opinion, was my biggest contribution to the company (Leap Game Studio). It was the first company IP I worked on, and the only one that got released during my time there. It is basically an infinite vertical jumper, where you have to avoid hazards, collect coins and fight enemies till you drop below the lava. During the whole duration of the project, 3 programmers where involved being me the only constant one. My main contributions to the project where the battle system, the level editor tool, most of the core gameplay, various hazards, UI implementation, game services (Game Center and Google Play) and language localization. Stores Google Play Itunes Technology Used Unity3D Texture Packer

posts.title: Bulldosis
posts.text: Bulldosis is a game for IOS/Android developed for the Peruvian representation of CAT. The game is very simple, it consists of an infinitely long slope, which gets steeper by the second until it reaches its steepest point. You have to tap as fast as you can to keep the bulldozer moving up, the furthest you go, the higher your score is. Also, the steeper the slope, the faster you have to tap to keep the bulldozer on screen. This game was developed while working on Leap Game Studio, I was the only programmer during the whole process. Stores Google Play Itunes Technology Used Unity3D

posts.title: Niusgames
posts.text: One of the things we liked doing on Leap Game Studio, was to make newsgames based on current events in our country, during my time working there, I had the chance to work on 3 different games, all of them were developed in under 12 hours with minor updates on the subsequent days. Two out of three of those are optimized to run on mobiles, with Twitter and Facebook sharing. As the games were supposed to be accessible, we used HTML5 to have a wide range of possible players, so no matter the device, the game worked. Game Links Nuestros Héroes Habla Mudo (We didn’t think about 60+ Hz monitor, so if you are in one of those, you are gonna have a bad time.) Plagio Como Cancha Technology Used Pahser

posts.title: Chiplay
posts.text: I worked on this game while on my first paid job, it was part of an ad campaign for a bubblegum brand.The aim of the client was to make the brand popular again by distributing codes printed inside the package that could be used as in-game currency. To fit the model, we developed a game similar to Worms, with 24 different weapons and 10 different hats to buy. The initial release of the game featured online multiplayer, but because the experience was in its majority unstable due to server capability, we dropped the multiplayer and added minigames. The campaign was a success for a couple of months, but without further updates, it went down rather quickly. Technology Used Unity3D Spine GAF

posts.title: MGX Fishing App
posts.text: This was my first time diving into deep mobile application development.I decided to use Xamarin in order to minimize development time based on the ability to deploy to both IOS and Android using almost the same code base.As I am not a designer, I used a UIKit known as Grial to facilitate the UI experience on the application. The application is somewhat a social network for fishermen based on the community formed by the store that hired me for the development of the app.It shows a feed where the users can share their captures, a map showing the best fishing spots pointed by the store, a feed where you can organize fishing getaways and some other minor features such as user profiles and a fish catalogue. By request of the client, some features were blocked by a 9 digit code, the code was randomly generated and given to the store manager for them to distribute among their clients.The application backend was developed around the already established server architecture, so it uses PHP and MySQL. You can find more in the respective store pages: Stores: Google Play Itunes

posts.title: About Me
posts.text: Hi!My name is Alvaro Barúa and I am a 24 years old Peruvian game developer. I started game development as a hobby back in 2011 when I started university, in 2014 I landed my first professional game development job in the Peruvian games industry, whereas small, this was a game changer for my career. I currently hold a Bachelors’ degree from Universidad Peruana de Ciencias Aplicadas in Computer Sciences, from which I graduated first of my class. As of today, I live in Newcastle UK, studying an MSc in Computer Game Engineering from Newcastle University.
